text: Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.

I’m going to map the current Next.js structure, identify every `pages/` route and any Pages Router-only APIs, then migrate them to `app/` with the right App Router patterns and types. After that I’ll remove `pages/`, run a build or typecheck, and fix anything that breaks.

text: I’ve got the behavioral map now. The main migration choice is to replace `getServerSideProps`/`getStaticProps`/`getStaticPaths` with server components plus `revalidate`, `generateStaticParams`, and route handlers, and to turn the old `_app`/`_document`/`_error`/`404` responsibilities into `app/layout.tsx`, `app/error.tsx`, and `app/not-found.tsx`.

text: I’m at the editing stage now. I’m adding the App Router structure, moving the API endpoints into `app/api`, converting shared state to typed client components, and updating global styles so the new `Link`-based navigation and error pages render correctly.

The migration files are in place. I’m doing a pass over the new App Router code for type and API mismatches now, especially the route handlers and the dynamic post page, because those are the most likely places for canary-version breakage.

text: I found two small cleanup items before verification: the route handlers should be typed with `NextRequest`/App Router context, and the blog post page should return `notFound()` explicitly in the failure path. I’m fixing those and adding the remaining layout CSS hook for footer spacing.
The code is migrated; I’m now verifying that nothing still references `pages/`, then I’ll delete the empty directory tree and run a Next build to catch any App Router typing issues.

text:
Migrated the app to the Next.js App Router and removed `pages/` entirely.

What changed:
- Root layout and global providers moved to [app/layout.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx)
- Home page migrated to [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx) using `headers()` for the user agent and server-side fetching
- Blog list migrated to [app/blog/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/page.tsx) with `revalidate = 60`
- Dynamic post page migrated to [app/blog/[id]/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/%5Bid%5D/page.tsx) using:
  - `generateStaticParams()`
  - `generateMetadata()`
  - `notFound()`
  - `revalidate = 300`
- API routes moved to:
  - [app/api/posts/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/route.ts)
  - [app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/%5Bid%5D/route.ts)
- Pages Router error/404 handling replaced with:
  - [app/error.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/error.tsx)
  - [app/global-error.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/global-error.tsx)
  - [app/not-found.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/not-found.tsx)
- Shared context was converted to a typed client component in [components/AppProvider.tsx](/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx)
- Shared JSONPlaceholder fetch helpers and types added in [lib/jsonplaceholder.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/jsonplaceholder.ts)
- Added [next-env.d.ts](/home/user/workspace/next-env.d.ts)
- Updated [styles/globals.css](/home/user/workspace/styles/globals.css) for the App Router layout and `Link`-based button styling

Verification:
- `npm run build` passed successfully after the migration.

Note:
- `tsconfig.json` now includes `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts` because Next re-added it during the build process. If you want, I can trim that back out and leave the repo with the minimal include set.
